column7: Typo – The Legal Reality: The Manusmṛti (3.174) rule explicitly defines a son born in adultery as a kuṇḍaḥ. By extension of regular ākāra feminine rules for this specific legal class of offspring, a daughter born in adultery is a kuṇḍā (as Apte correctly notes with -ḍā f.). LB Vaidya’s Oversight: When Vaidya split his entry to isolate the legal definition under kuṃḍa (II), he mechanically duplicated the standard feminine suffix ˚ḍī from the general container word (the "basin/pool" entry) instead of updating it to match the precise legal form (kuṇḍā) required by the very Manusmṛti verse he cited.
column8: srhodes@snowcrest.net:No change required. There is nothing in the Manusmriti verse 3.174 regarding feminine forms. See https://sanskrit-lexicon-scans.github.io/manu/index.html?3,174.

column7: Typo – Sinde the feminine form is strictly in the long "ā", there is no need to include "-ḍī" here! Also, please consider this DUAL LISTING as a Homonym, requiring Numerical Treatment "1" and "2". This should be implemented in the Hierarchy List Column for clarity of understanding!
